name: Beef Stew with Shiitake Mushrooms and Baby Vegetables
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds trimmed boneless beef chuck, cut into 1 1/2-inch cubes
12 ounces baby pattypan squash, halved (12 to 14)
1 pound fresh shiitake mushrooms, stemmed and caps thickly sliced
3 tablespoons chopped fresh marjoram, or 1 tablespoon dried
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the flour in a baking pan. Season with salt and pepper.
2. Melt 4 tablespoons butter in a heavy large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
- Working in batches, coat the meat with flour; add to the pot and brown on all sides.
- Using a slotted spoon, transfer the meat to a plate.
3. Melt 2 tablespoons butter in the same pot over medium-high heat.
- Add the onions and sauté until tender, about 6 minutes.
- Mix in the tomato paste, then wine. Bring to a boil, scraping up any browned bits.
- Add broth and sugar, then beef and any accumulated juices. Bring to a boil.
- Reduce heat, cover partially and simmer for 1 1/2 hours.
4. Add potatoes and carrots; simmer uncovered until meat and vegetables are almost tender, about 25 minutes.
5. Add squash; simmer until almost tender, about 10 minutes.
6. Add mushrooms and 3 tablespoons marjoram; simmer until mushrooms are tender, about 5 minutes.
7. Season with salt and pepper. (Can be made 1 day ahead. Cool slightly. Cover, chill. Before serving, cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally.)
